findings.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/static/js/upload.js:216 — uploadErrors.innerHTML with unescaped user-supplied filename data; createPhotoCard() inserts photo.originalFileName directly into HTML template literal via insertAdjacentHTML() without escaping

findings.name: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
findings.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before it is used as an identifier for a resource that may be outside the intended sphere of control.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/controller/PhotoFileController.java — @PathVariable String id passed directly to photoService.getPhotoById(id) with only null/empty check; no UUID format validation performed before database lookup

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/service/impl/PhotoServiceImpl.java:72 — file type validation relies solely on client-supplied Content-Type header (file.getContentType()) which can be spoofed; no server-side magic-byte/content inspection performed

findings.name: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/controller/DetailController.java — photo deletion does not log requester IP or user identity; src/main/java/com/photoalbum/controller/HomeController.java — upload operations do not log source IP; no authentication/authorization failure events are logged

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties — spring.datasource.username=photoalbum, spring.datasource.password=photoalbum hardcoded; src/main/resources/application-docker.properties — same credentials hardcoded
